title: Eczema / dermatitis

red_flags.text: Lists non-blanching rash, rapidly worsening painful eczema, fever or lethargy, respiratory distress, clustered blisters, punched-out erosions, recurrent infection, pregnancy, and psychosocial concerns.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped fever, discharge or infection concern, rapid worsening, severe flare, sleep, face or eye, and return fields.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists non-blanching rash, rapidly worsening painful eczema, fever or lethargy, respiratory distress, clustered blisters, punched-out erosions, recurrent infection, pregnancy, and psychosocial concerns.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Atopic Dermatitis

investigations.text: Supports morphology and distribution review, atopy history, dry skin, selected non-routine tests, and differential-diagnosis assessment.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped morphology, distribution, atopy, prior history, and selected clinician-reviewed assessment fields.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports morphology and distribution review, atopy history, dry skin, selected non-routine tests, and differential-diagnosis assessment.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Atopic Dermatitis

assessment.text: Covers itch, dry skin, erythema, vesicles, crusting, scaling, excoriation, lichenification, fissuring, age-linked distribution, atopy, sleep, and quality-of-life impact.
assessment.rationale: Supports mapped atopic-dermatitis terminology, itch, dry skin, flare, atopy, allergy, sleep, distribution, and morphology fields.
assessment.evidence_extract: Covers itch, dry skin, erythema, vesicles, crusting, scaling, excoriation, lichenification, fissuring, age-linked distribution, atopy, sleep, and quality-of-life impact.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Atopic Dermatitis

medication.text: Covers clinician-led trigger reduction, skin-barrier care, education, and pharmacological choices; no medicine dose is mapped.
medication.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-documented emollient, skin care, trigger, medicine, flare-plan, education, question, and follow-up fields without doses.
medication.evidence_extract: Covers clinician-led trigger reduction, skin-barrier care, education, and pharmacological choices; no medicine dose is mapped.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Atopic Dermatitis

source_limitations: Allergic dermatitis as a separate diagnosis, discharge-specific culture review, immunocompromise, mucosal or wound fields, fixed two-to-four-week review timing, and dose-bearing medicines remain unsupported.
Atopic dermatitis remains clinician-stated and severity or infection must not be inferred automatically.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
